title: remote: podman-remote build is very slow
body:
### Issue Description

If there are large files or directories in the directory where the `podman-remote build` is executed,
the processing time of the `podman-remote build` will increase in proportion to the size.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Make a Containerfile.
2. Execute `podman-remote build .` command.

### Describe the results you received


| size | local | remote |
| ---- | ---- | ---- |
| 0.0G | 0m0.104s | 0m0.122s |
| 1.0G | 0m0.047s | 0m4.009s |
| 2.0G | 0m2.276s | 0m11.236s |
| 3.0G | 0m0.043s | 0m25.850s |

<details>
<summary>0.0G</summary>

```
[root@fedora38 testdir]# ls -l
total 4
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 29 Aug 10 15:13 Containerfile

[root@fedora38 testdir]# cat Containerfile
FROM registry.redhat.io/ubi9

[root@fedora38 testdir]# time podman build .
STEP 1/1: FROM registry.redhat.io/ubi9
COMMIT
--> 05936a40cfa4
05936a40cfa48169c64b8c4227a453f439d933376d0fa570ceca25e2124f120d

real    0m0.104s
user    0m0.032s
sys     0m0.027s

[root@fedora38 testdir]# time podman-remote build .
STEP 1/1: FROM registry.redhat.io/ubi9
COMMIT
--> 05936a40cfa4
05936a40cfa48169c64b8c4227a453f439d933376d0fa570ceca25e2124f120d

real    0m0.122s
user    0m0.034s
sys     0m0.024s
```
</details>

```
total 3.0G
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root    29 Aug 10 15:13 Containerfile

```
</details>

### Describe the results you expected

`podman build` should be as fast as the local environment.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

`podman-remote build -f ./Containerfile` is also very slow.

```
[root@fedora38 testdir]# time podman-remote build -f ./Containerfile
STEP 1/1: FROM registry.redhat.io/ubi9
COMMIT
--> 05936a40cfa4
05936a40cfa48169c64b8c4227a453f439d933376d0fa570ceca25e2124f120d

real    0m23.845s
user    0m8.648s
sys     0m0.706s
```

comments.body:
You can use `.containerignore` to avoid sending big files in the build context

https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html#containerignore-dockerignore

This is normal, build must send to complete build context as tar to the server, so it will spend most time creating the tar on the client then extracting it on the server again.

For local it doesn't matter as we can just look up files as needed
